fact: May 14 is exactly 134 days into the year, which means there are exactly 231 days left until New Year's Eve!

In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby rabbits to be hopping around - they're born with their eyes closed but by mid-May they're usually bouncing everywhere like furry ping pong balls!
The sun often rises around 5:30 AM in many northern places on May 14, which means if you're awake that early, you get to see the sky turn from deep purple to bright orange in just 20 minutes!
May 14 falls in the middle of Taurus season, so anyone born on this day is supposedly as stubborn as a bull but also loves snacks and cozy blankets.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, May 14 is when leaves are typically turning brilliant reds and golds - it's like nature is showing off with the world's biggest art project!
This date is exactly 35 days after the spring equinox in the north, which means daylight is getting longer by about 2 minutes every single day!
May 14 is often peak blooming time for lilacs in many northern regions - these purple flowers smell so strong they can make your nose tingle from 20 feet away!

In many temperate places, May 14 typically marks the time when morning dew is heaviest because warm days and cool nights create the perfect recipe for grass that looks like it's covered in tiny diamonds!
The moon phase on May 14 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Flower Moon because everything is blooming like crazy!
May 14 is when many fish species start their spawning season, which means underwater it's like the world's weirdest dance party with millions of fish doing their wiggliest moves!
In northern regions, May 14 is often when the last patches of snow finally give up and melt, creating tiny waterfalls and streams that sound like nature's own water park!
This date falls during the time when Earth is moving through space at about 67,000 miles per hour in its orbit - you're basically on the fastest roller coaster in the universe right now!
